task: Write a Python function token_case(s) taking an underscore_delimited identifier and returning it in camel case: the first token lowercased, every later token capitalized, and the tokens joined with no separator. Tokens that appear in the exception list ['URL', 'ID'] keep their exact given form wherever they appear. An exception token keeps its exact form even when it is the first token. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: Most criteria are met, however the edge cases could be more thoroughly addressed

trace: The function takes an underscore-delimited identifier as its input, checks for exceptions (URL and ID), converts the tokens to camel case by capitalizing every later token except for the first and keeps any exception tokens in their exact form. If there are no exceptions, it returns the camel cased string without leading underscores. An edge case handled is when an exception token appears as the first token of the identifier.
